mission.title: Install and Configure Trivy Operator (Aqua Security)
mission.description: Trivy Operator is a Kubernetes-native security toolkit that automates the process of scanning container images and Kubernetes resources for vulnerabilities. It is useful for ensuring the security of your applications by providing continuous vulnerability assessments. Key trade-offs include the need for additional resources and potential performance impacts during scanning.

steps.title: Add the Aqua Security Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Aqua Security Helm repository to your Helm client, allowing you to install the Trivy Operator from it.

```bash

helm repo add aqua https://aquasecurity.github.io/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Aqua Security Helm charts repository.
> This updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Trivy Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Trivy Operator in the 'trivy-system' namespace using Helm.

```bash

kubectl create namespace trivy-system

helm install trivy-operator aqua/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.23.0

```

> Creates a namespace for the Trivy Operator.
> Installs the Trivy Operator with a specific version.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Trivy Operator pods are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

```

> Lists all pods in the 'trivy-system' namespace to verify the installation.

steps.title: Create a TrivyConfiguration custom resource
steps.description:
This step creates a TrivyConfiguration resource to configure the scanning behavior of the Trivy Operator.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1
kind: TrivyConfiguration
metadata:
  name: example-trivyconfig
  namespace: trivy-system
spec:
  image: 'docker.io/aquasec/trivy:0.21.0'
  severity: 'HIGH'
EOF

```

> Creates a sample TrivyConfiguration resource.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1
kind: TrivyConfiguration
metadata:
  name: example-trivyconfig
  namespace: trivy-system
spec:
  image: 'docker.io/aquasec/trivy:0.21.0'
  severity: 'HIGH'
EOF

steps.title: Verify the TrivyConfiguration
steps.description:
This step checks if the TrivyConfiguration resource is created and ready.

```bash

kubectl get trivyconfigurations -n trivy-system

```

> Lists all TrivyConfiguration resources in the 'trivy-system' namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Trivy Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Trivy Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete the TrivyConfiguration and any associated data.

```bash

helm uninstall trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system

kubectl delete namespace trivy-system

```

> Uninstalls the Trivy Operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Trivy Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup any important configurations. This step upgrades the Trivy Operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get trivyconfigurations -n trivy-system -o yaml > trivyconfig-backup.yaml

helm upgrade trivy-operator aqua/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.23.0

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

```

> Backup existing TrivyConfiguration resources.
> Upgrades the Trivy Operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get trivyconfigurations -n trivy-system -o yaml > trivyconfig-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'trivy-operator: no such file or directory'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Helm chart may not be installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Re-run the Helm install command and ensure the repository is added correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'TrivyConfiguration not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The TrivyConfiguration resource may not have been created or applied correctly.

**Fix:**
Check the YAML syntax and reapply the configuration using kubectl.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'trivy-system' namespace are in 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be an issue with the Trivy image or configuration.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n trivy-system' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'permission denied' when accessing resources
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** RBAC permissions may not be set correctly for the Trivy Operator.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the necessary RBAC roles and bindings are created for the Trivy Operator.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for custom image scanning policies and enhanced reporting features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.22, review the new configuration options for custom policies.

resolution.summary: You should see the Trivy Operator pods running in the 'trivy-system' namespace with a status of 'Running'. The output of 'kubectl get pods -n trivy-system' should show the pods in a healthy state.

kind: TrivyConfiguration
metadata:

  namespace: trivy-system

  image: 'docker.io/aquasec/trivy:0.21.0'

EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
